data.title: Research infinite canvas approaches & build prototype

data.body: Fetch and analyze: Codrops infinite canvas tutorial, ywian step-by-step tutorial, Sandro Maglione HTML canvas, p5.js pan/zoom gist, p5.canvascam library. Build a minimal pan/zoom prototype with 5-10 test items. Determine best approach: CSS transforms vs Canvas2D vs p5.js. See notes/handoff-infinite-canvas.md for full context.

Plan comprehensively rewritten by planning agent. Analyzed 5 external resources (Codrops Three.js infinite canvas, ywian Canvas2D tutorial, Sandro HTML zoom/pan, p5.js pan/zoom gist, p5.canvascam library). Evaluated 4 approaches: CSS transforms (RECOMMENDED), Canvas2D, p5.js, WebGL/Three.js. Plan covers: research findings, approach evaluation, recommended architecture (CSS transform container + lerp easing + pointer events), pan/zoom/pinch algorithms with code, layout algorithm interface, popover reuse, performance strategy, mobile considerations, accessibility, implementation sequence mapping to ASMV-61 through ASMV-65, acceptance criteria, risk assessment. Ready for implementation sub-agent.

REVIEW: Code review of infinite canvas prototype (ASMV-60).

VERDICT: PASS — prototype meets all 10 acceptance criteria. Minor issues noted below for downstream tasks; none block the prototype.

== Acceptance Criteria (Section 5) ==
All 10 criteria verified and passing:
1. Pan works (pointer events + setPointerCapture) ✓
2. Zoom toward cursor (standard world-coord algorithm) ✓
3. Lerp easing (dual-state camera, rAF with convergence detection) ✓
4. Real entries rendered (server-loaded via getAllNowEntries, filtered to image entries) ✓
5. Layout is simple (seeded PRNG scatter — actually exceeds prototype scope, implements collision avoidance meant for ASMV-61) ✓
6. No new dependencies (package.json unchanged) ✓
7. Design language (bg-paper, grain-texture, artifact-treatment, font-serif/mono/sans) ✓
8. Server/client split (now/page.tsx server component, InfiniteCanvas client) ✓
9. Videos autoplay muted+loop+playsInline ✓
10. TypeScript passes clean, lint has no new warnings ✓

== Scope Notes ==
The implementation goes beyond the prototype spec in two ways:
- canvas-layout.ts includes a full collision-avoidance layout algorithm (ASMV-61 scope). This is fine — it works and saves future effort.
- Pinch zoom is implemented (ASMV-63 scope). It has a math bug (see below) but doesn't affect mouse interaction.
- Popover detail view is implemented (ASMV-64 scope). Clean, reuses ArtifactBar pattern.

== Bug: Pinch Zoom Double-Applies Midpoint Shift ==
In InfiniteCanvas.tsx lines 240-249, the zoom-toward-midpoint calculation already adjusts camera offset to keep the midpoint stable. Lines 248-249 then additionally add midpoint delta for panning, which double-counts the shift and will cause canvas drift during two-finger gestures. Fix for ASMV-63: remove lines 248-249, or restructure to separate the zoom offset adjustment from the pan offset adjustment.

== Minor Issues (non-blocking, for downstream tasks) ==
1. MIN_SCALE/MAX_SCALE are 0.15/3.0 vs plan's 0.2/2.5. Intentional prototype tuning — fine.
2. entryMap (line 303) created fresh each render. Negligible at 37 items; useMemo would be cleaner.
3. artifact-treatment on button: next/image's fill mode wraps img in a span, so .artifact-treatment > img selector won't match. Sepia filter only applies to videos. Pre-existing pattern from ArtifactBar — fix when doing visual polish.
4. Duplicate isVideo helper in InfiniteCanvas.tsx and ArtifactBar.tsx. Extract to shared util in polish pass.
5. No role=dialog or aria-modal on popover. Explicitly out of scope per plan Section 5.

== Design/UX Concern (for human review) ==
Text-only NowEntries (those without an image field) are filtered out by now/page.tsx line 21-22 and become invisible. The previous vertical list rendered all entries including their MDX content. If any entries exist without images, they will silently disappear. This is an intentional trade-off of the canvas metaphor (only visual artifacts on the desk), but worth confirming with Julianna.

Review (post-implementation, fresh read):

PASS — all 10 acceptance criteria met.

Findings:
1. Pan: pointer events with setPointerCapture, incremental delta tracking. Pan deltas not divided by scale BUT this is correct because translate() is applied before scale() in the transform string — screen-space pixels map 1:1 regardless of zoom level.
2. Zoom: world-coordinate conversion before scale change, offset adjustment to keep cursor-world point stable. Math is correct.
3. Lerp: dual-state (current + target) with factor 0.12, rAF stops when converged (epsilon 0.01). Clean.
4. Layout: mulberry32 PRNG seeded from slug hash, rejection sampling for overlap avoidance. World 3000x2500, items 200-400px. Deterministic.
5. Build passes clean (verified after .next cache clear).

Minor notes (not blocking):
- Items are square (height = width) with object-cover — real images get cropped. ASMV-61 should handle aspect ratio-aware sizing.
- Footer is hidden behind the fixed canvas — intentional for immersive view but worth revisiting in ASMV-65 polish.
- page.tsx homepage change (studio desk → link to /now) was pre-existing in working tree, not from this task.

Verdict: prototype scope met. Ready for commit.
